step_a.description: Forward verification: 15+ factual claims from PART2 about CI/CD stages, test types, migration phases, API contracts

details.claim: PART2 §6.3: Test pyramid 3-layer (Unit 80%+, Integration 60%+, E2E 100% core)
details.evidence: B5 §1.1 defines identical pyramid: Unit 80%+, Integration 60%+, E2E 핵심 시나리오 100%

details.claim: PART2 §6.3: Python Unit ~50 files, Rust ~8, React ~15, Integration ~14, E2E ~8
details.evidence: B5 details individual test files consistent with these counts. Python unit tests span §2.1.1-2.1.6 (schemas 11 + orange_core 6 + blue_nodes 6 + safety 7 + storage 5 + agent 10 = ~45-50). Rust §2.2 = 8 tests. React §2.3 = 9 mandatory + extensions ~15.
B5 §1.3 defines exact same pattern: T-{계층}-{문서ID}-{순번}, layers U/I/E, doc D2~D8/SYS

details.claim: PART2 §6.3: AC mapping 50 AC -> 79 test cases (B5 §7.3 정본)
details.evidence: B5 §5 provides detailed AC-to-test mapping across D2-D8, consistent with 50 AC to 79 tests

details.claim: PART2 §6.4: 14 CI workflow files listed (quality-python/rust/react/schema, test-python/rust/react, coverage-report, build-tauri, release, security, build-docker, deploy-v2, deploy-v3)
details.evidence: PART2 lists 14 separate workflow files. PHASE_B6 §1 defines a single ci.yml integrated workflow structure. PART2 L4840 NOTE (XREF-V0-19) explicitly acknowledges this difference: 'PHASE_B6 정본은 ci.yml 단일 통합 워크플로우. PART2는 가독성을 위해 역할별 분리 표기.' This was already found as F-81 in Phase 2.

details.claim: PART2 §6.4: CI/CD evolves V1(local+CI) -> V2(CI/CD auto Docker) -> V3(CI/CD+staging K8s ArgoCD)
details.evidence: B6 §1.1 table defines identical evolution: V1=로컬+CI, V2=CI/CD 자동화 Docker, V3=CI/CD+스테이징 K8s

details.claim: PART2 §6.4: Tech stack = GitHub Actions, 3 languages (Python/Rust/TypeScript)
details.evidence: B6 meta: 'GitHub Actions, Tauri 2.0, Python 3.11+, Rust (stable), Node 20+, Docker'. 3 languages confirmed.

details.claim: PART2 §6.13: Total coding tasks ~696 (V0~41, V1~284, V2~292, V3~79)
details.evidence: The §6.13 summary table is PART2's own aggregation. No SOT document independently defines total coding task counts.
PART2 states 16 items synchronized with CLAUDE.md §10. Items include config, schema, module skeleton generation, cost LOCK, etc.

details.claim: PART2 §7.2: V1 GO/NO-GO = 22 items including test infra (80%+), CI/CD (8-stage), EventTypeRegistry
details.evidence: V1 #13 requires test infra 80%+ (B5 alignment), #14 requires CI/CD GitHub Actions 8-stage (B6 alignment). 22 items listed.

details.claim: PART2 §7.3: V2 GO/NO-GO = 14 items including SQLite->Postgres, Chroma->Qdrant, NetworkX->Neo4j migration
details.evidence: B7 §1.1 defines exact migration path: SQLite->Postgres, Chroma(embedded)->Qdrant(server), JSON->Neo4j. PART2 §7.3 #5-7 match.

details.claim: PART2 §7.5.3: Performance targets simple<=2s, complex<=10s, self-check<=1s
details.evidence: PART2 L6175-6179 lists identical targets. Phase 2 F-04 noted these were missing from PART2, but they ARE present in §7.5.3.

details.claim: PART2 V0-STEP-4: CostGate thresholds 70% warn, 85% escalate, 95% block (P30-058 3단계)
details.evidence: PART2 L1097 V0-STEP-4 prompt says '70% warn, 85% escalate, 95% block (P30-058 3단계)'. But the LOCK value (D2.0-07 §4.2, CLAUDE.md §20) is 80% warn / 100% block (2-stage). The V0-STEP-4 prompt uses a different 3-stage threshold from PLAN-3.0 which is older than the LOCK. PART2 V1-Phase 5 prompt (L2453) correctly says '80% warn, 100% block'. Internal inconsistency within PART2 prompts.

details.claim: PART2 §7.5.5: LOCK values list includes approval.timeout_s=600, circuit_breaker.recovery_time_sec=60, semantic_cache.threshold cosine>=0.95
details.evidence: All three LOCK values confirmed in SOT: approval 600s (D2.0-07 §3), CB 60s (D2.0-05 §4.4 LOCK), semantic cache 0.95 (CLAUDE.md §20).

details.claim: PART2 §6.11: EventTypeRegistry 123 items from D2.1-D2 + 11 cl.rt.* = 134 total
details.evidence: The exact 123 count in D2.1-D2 requires counting all event types. The PART2 comment attempts a breakdown (oc.*33 + wf.*4 + ... = 122~123). The 11 cl.rt.* items are PART2 additions for RT-BNP not present in D2.1-D2 SOT. Cannot verify exact 123 count without full D2.1-D2 enumeration.
PART2 L5708-5718 lists 36 FailureCode items. Count verified: I-1(2) + I-2(4) + I-3(3) + I-4(3) + I-5(5) + General(4) + FM(4) + OC(5) + TL(3) + MC(3) = 36.
PART2 L5723-5731 lists 23 Fallback items. Mapping table (L5738-5785) covers all 36 FC->FB mappings.
This is a self-referential claim. The 15 SC items are documented with resolution decisions, but there is no external SOT that independently validates the count or resolutions.

step_b.description: Reverse verification: 10+ critical facts from B5/B6/B7 SOTs that must appear in PART2

details.sot_fact: B5 §1.2: 5 test principles (AC complete mapping, schema-first, secret separation, reproducibility, failure tracking)
details.evidence: PART2 §6.3 AC mapping section explicitly references B5 §7.3. Schema-first testing is implicit in V0-STEP-6 prompt (25 schema tests). Reproducibility through CI/CD.

details.sot_fact: B5 §2.4: Pydantic schema validation CI step (37 schemas import test)
details.evidence: PART2 §6.4 lists quality-schema.yml as one of 14 workflows. V0-STEP-6 prompt includes 25-schema validation tests.
PART2 does not explicitly document the branch strategy. Already identified as F-82 in Phase 2.

details.sot_fact: B6 §3.4: Coverage thresholds - Python 75% min/85% target, Rust 80%, React 80%
details.evidence: PART2 §6.3 states 80%+ for all three languages. PART2 uses 80% as the unified target. B6 §3.4 has Python minimum at 75% (lower). Minor simplification but not a conflict.

details.sot_fact: B6 §4.3: Docker service matrix (vamos-orange-core, vamos-blue-nodes, vamos-api-gateway)
details.evidence: PART2 V2 Docker Compose (L2817-2826) lists different service names: vamos-app, postgres, qdrant, neo4j, redis, timescaledb. The B6 §4.3 specific service names are not used. Already identified as F-83 in Phase 2.

details.sot_fact: B7 §1.2: 6 migration principles (Zero Data Loss, Rollback Always, Verify Before Commit, Backup First, Incremental Migration, Schema Version Pinning)
details.evidence: PART2 V2-Phase 1 prompt (L2769-2839) covers: batch migration (incremental), rollback/downgrade (rollback always), checksum verification (verify before commit), BackupConfigSchema (backup first). L2838 explicitly references 'PHASE_B7 §1.2 LOCK'.

details.sot_fact: B7 §1.1: Migration scope table V1->V2->V3 for 6 components (meta/index, logs, vector DB, graph DB, embedding, config)
details.evidence: PART2 V2-Phase 1 covers all 6: SQLite->Postgres (#1-2), Chroma->Qdrant (#3), JSON->Neo4j (#4), config v1->v2 (#5). Embedding model stays BGE-M3 in V1/V2, V3 uses text-embedding-3-small (consistent with B7).

details.sot_fact: B7 §2.1: 37 schemas with independent versions (SchemaVersion class with major/minor/patch)
details.evidence: PART2 V0-STEP-2 prompt mentions 25 initial schemas. B7 §2.1 lists 33 schemas with specific versions. The total reaches 37 including Phase-A additions. PART2 does not explicitly mention SchemaVersion class but references schema versioning in VAL-005.

details.sot_fact: B7 §2.3: Alembic migration script structure (migrations/versions/ + custom/ + utils/)
details.evidence: PART2 V2-Phase 1 prompt L2778 references 'Alembic 기반 스키마 마이그레이션'. The specific directory structure from B7 is not replicated in PART2 but the tool and approach match.

details.sot_fact: B7 §3.1: SQLite->Postgres migrator with TABLE_MAP, batch processing, validate_record_count, validate_sample_integrity
details.evidence: PART2 V2-Phase 1 prompt L2780-2783: 'batch 1000건 단위', '마이그레이션 전/후 행 수 일치 검증 + 체크섬 비교', 'rollback/downgrade 함수 필수 포함'. All key aspects covered.

details.sot_fact: B5 §4.2: E2E test scenarios 8 items (T-E-SYS-001 through T-E-SYS-008)
details.evidence: PART2 §6.3 lists ~8 E2E tests. B5 §4.2 lists exactly 8 scenarios including chat flow, cost downshift, cost block, HITL approval, guardrails block, error recovery, MCP bridge, config change.

details.sot_fact: B1 §0: API contract rules (trace_id mandatory, vamos:{category}:{action} naming, MCP Streamable HTTP LOCK, error/success response standards)
details.evidence: PART2 V0-STEP-3 prompt uses 'vamos:decision:create' naming (consistent). trace_id is enforced throughout. MCP Streamable HTTP is mentioned. However, the full B1 API contract rules (error response format, success response format) are not reproduced in PART2 prompts. Implementers must reference B1 directly.

dim_c.description: Implementation feasibility checks for CI/CD pipeline and migration path

details.check: CI/CD: GitHub Actions 14 workflows feasible with free tier?
details.reasoning: GitHub Actions free tier provides 2,000 minutes/month for private repos, 3,000 for public. 14 workflows triggered on PR/push is standard. Cross-platform Tauri builds (Win/Mac/Linux) will consume the most minutes. Feasible with judicious caching and conditional triggers.

details.check: Migration: SQLite->Postgres batch 1000 records feasible for V1 data volume?
details.reasoning: V1 is a local MVP with limited data. SQLite->Postgres migration via Alembic with batch processing is well-established. Record counts likely in thousands, not millions. Rollback via table DROP + SQLite restore is straightforward.

B7 §3.2 / PART2 V2-Phase 1 define 4-phase transition with 30-day dual-collection parallel run. Re-embedding with BGE-M3 is computationally intensive but feasible for V1-scale data. HNSW parameters (ef_construct=128, m=16) are standard.

details.check: Testing: 80%+ coverage achievable for 3-language polyglot project?
details.reasoning: 80%+ is achievable for Python (pytest mature) and React (vitest standard). Rust Tauri code with IPC handlers may be harder to unit test in isolation (Tauri runtime dependency). B6 §3.4 sets Python minimum at 75%, suggesting 80% is aspirational. Coverage for Rust IPC may require integration-level testing rather than pure unit testing.

details.check: V3 K8s deployment: Helm + Blue-Green + ArgoCD feasible at $200/month LOCK?
details.reasoning: PART2 provides Hetzner CX31 + RunPod alternative at ~$123-142/month. Full K8s cluster (AWS/GCP) would exceed $200/month easily. The Hetzner path uses Docker Compose (not true K8s). The document acknowledges this trade-off. True K8s with GPU nodes requires careful resource management.

details.check: GO/NO-GO checklists: V0(16) + V1(22) + V2(14) + V3(12) = 64 items measurable?
details.reasoning: All items have concrete criteria: module counts, test coverage percentages, cost limits, TC measurement mechanisms (§7.2-7.4). TC measurement tables provide specific tools (qod_tracker.py, rag_benchmark.py), periods, and judgment criteria.

details.check: V1->V2 transition conditions measurable? (QoD>=0.85, RAG>=60%, memory error<1%)
details.reasoning: PART2 §7.2 TC measurement table (L6074-6081) defines specific tools (qod_tracker.py, rag_benchmark.py with RAGAS, memory_audit.py), periods (daily/weekly), and judgment criteria. All conditions are quantifiable and automatable.

findings.description: CostGate threshold inconsistency within PART2 prompts. V0-STEP-4 prompt uses 3-stage (70%/85%/95%) from PLAN-3.0, while V1-Phase 5 and §7.5.5 correctly use 2-stage LOCK (80%/100%). The V0-STEP-4 prompt would cause AI to generate code with wrong thresholds.
findings.recommendation: Update V0-STEP-4 prompt CostGate thresholds to 80%/100% LOCK values

findings.description: Branch strategy (main/develop/feature/hotfix/release) from PHASE_B6 §1.3 not documented in PART2. Confirmed finding from Phase 2 F-82.
findings.recommendation: Add branch strategy reference note in §6.4

findings.description: CI workflow structure: PART2 lists 14 individual workflow files vs B6 single ci.yml. PART2 NOTE (XREF-V0-19) acknowledges this. Confirmed finding from Phase 2 F-81.
findings.recommendation: Already mitigated by XREF note. No action needed.

findings.description: Docker service names from B6 §4.3 (vamos-orange-core, vamos-blue-nodes, vamos-api-gateway) not used in PART2 Docker Compose definition. PART2 uses different service names (vamos-app, postgres, etc.). Confirmed finding from Phase 2 F-83.
findings.recommendation: Reconcile service naming between B6 and PART2 V2 Docker Compose

findings.description: B6 §3.4 Python coverage minimum threshold is 75% while PART2 §6.3 uses 80%+ uniformly. The B6 CI script checks --min-python 75 (L521). This is a stricter standard in PART2 vs SOT, not a conflict, but could cause false CI failures if B6 threshold is adopted.
findings.recommendation: Clarify whether 75% or 80% is the enforced CI gate for Python

findings.description: PART2 §7.5 cross-cut review items provide thorough verification framework: document consistency (7 checks), security review (5 checks), performance review (5 checks), LOCK value verification (10 items), SOURCE_CONFLICT index (15 items). This is well-structured and comprehensive.
